source_title: DOW-UAP-D44, Range Fouler Reporting Form, Gulf of Aden, October 2020
description_blurb:
This document is a Range Fouler Reporting Form, a standardized reporting form the U.S. Navy uses to record the circumstances surrounding an unauthorized intrusion into controlled airspace during active military operations or training. These reports contain a narrative description of the observer’s experiences. 
A U.S. military operator reported observing a “round, cold object” via infrared sensor, traveling at 319 degrees (northwest) at approximately 20 mph. The report describes the UAP making “abrupt directional changes” during the event.
All descriptive and estimative language contained in this report reflects the reporter’s subjective interpretation at the time of the event. Such characterizations should not be interpreted as a conclusive indication of the presence or absence of any intrinsic object features or performance characteristics.
title: DOW-UAP-D44, Range Fouler Reporting Form, Gulf of Aden, October 2020
summary: A U.S. military operator reported observing a "round, cold object" via infrared sensor over the Gulf of Aden on October 15, 2020, from 14:18:39Z to 14:19:52Z. The object was tracked traveling at 319 degrees (northwest) at approximately 20 mph and made a few abrupt directional changes during the 1-minute contact. The IR sensor was set to black hot, and the object appeared bright white. The sensor was aimed -50 degrees below the aircraft's altitude, with a slant range of 4.06NM and a ground range of 4.78KM.
reported_object_description: A round, cold object, appearing bright white in infrared (black hot setting).
reported_behavior: Traveling at 319 degrees (northwest) at 20 mph, making abrupt directional changes.
bust_reasoning: The bust score is 45 because the document describes an object with unusual features ('round, cold object' making 'abrupt directional changes' at 20 mph) that resist easy, common prosaic explanations like a simple weather balloon without further details. The 'cold' signature in IR is specific. However, it is a single-sensor (IR) observation with a single witness, and the speed (20 mph) is relatively low, making more advanced kinematic anomalies less likely. A mundane explanation like a small drone or even a large bird remains plausible, but the document does not definitively identify one, placing it in the 'genuinely uncertain' range.
full_text:
# DOW-UAP-D44, Range Fouler Reporting Form, Gulf of Aden, October 2020 - Page 1

[Stamp: Declassified by MG Richard A. Harrison USCENTCOM Chief of Staff Declassified on 28 March 2026]

[Note: Please do not use the purple "submit" button above. Save and email the complete file manually (see below).]

# Range Fouler Reporting Form

Please complete this form to the best of your ability. If you do not have the requested information, please leave the field blank. If there was more than one "group," please report each on a separate form for data collection purposes. You should receive a response within 5 business days.

| Last Name, First Name | Rank | Squadron | SIPR Email Address | Crew Position |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| [redacted] | O-2 | 172 ATKS | [redacted] | Other |

This information is for contact only. SPEAR sanitizes all reports of identifying information. Absolutely no identifying information for aircrew or squadron will be recorded for analysis.

| 10/15/20 | 14:18:39 | Day | [redacted] | [redacted] | ISR | [redacted] |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Date (mm/dd/yy)** | **Time of detection (hh:mm:ss Z)** | **Day / Night** | **Side No.** | **Buno** | **Mission Description (CAS, BFM, etc)** | **LFE?** |

| Contact Working Area (Ex: W-72 1A) | Contact Latitude (DD:MM:SS) | Contact Longitude (DD:MM:SS) | Contact Altitude (Ex: 22000) | Altitude Constant? | Wind Dir at Contact Alt (From) | Wind Speed |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| [redacted] | [redacted] | [redacted] | [redacted] | [redacted] | [redacted] | [redacted] |

Please attempt to determine a Lat / Long for the contact at initial detection. We recommend using JMPS to reference the sensor-derived bearing and range relative to the bullseye you had in use. If that method is not available, any reasonable derivation of location will work, but please be as specific as possible and make a note in the comments section. These locations may be used to cue other means of tracking.

**Was the contact moving?** Yes
**Direction/Speed (Ex. 090/15)** 261/30

***

**Radar Equipped:** [ ] | **AIM-9x Self-Track:** [ ] | **Please check all that apply:**
**Stable Trackfile?:** [ ] | **ATFLIR Autotrack:** [ ] | Round: [x] | Moving Parts: [ ]
**# of Contacts in "Group":** 1 | **Tally Achieved:** [ ] | Square: [ ] | Metallic: [ ]
**EA Indications (Check all that apply):** | Balloon-shaped: [ ] | Markings: [ ]
[ ] ECM Arc | [ ] Letter Identifier | [ ] False Trackfiles | [ ] Other/Ambiguous | Wings/Airframe: [ ] | Translucent: [ ]
| | | | | Other Shape: [ ] | Opaque: [ ]
| | | | | Apparent Propulsion: [ ] | Reflective: [ ]

***

Please use the field below to describe the contact and any interaction in your own words with as much detail as possible. Please be sure to include any detail not included in questions above.

Contact at 14:18:39Z to 14:19:52Z on 15OCT2020. MGRS location 40Q BD 63 1.4a 19 1.4a (estimated lat/long 1.4a). While at 19,073 HAT over the Gulf of Aden we tracked a round, cold object in IR traveling 319 degrees at 20 mph. It made a few abrupt directional changes during the 1 minute contact. Our sensor was aimed -50 degrees below our altitude with a slant range of 4.06NM and ground range of 4.78KM. The IR sensor was set to black hot and the object in question was a bright white.

| (b)(6) |
| :--- |

Don't use the purple "submit" button! Save this form with filename "Date_Squadron_RF.pdf" and email it to (b)(6) (Also in the global). For troubleshooting, call (b)(6)

Thank you for your time. Please ensure all display tapes are ripped for the entire time of interaction and saved as a .wmv (Example: 4 May VFA-106 HUD.wmv). Squadron intel personnel shall upload those files to the repository located at this link.

| (b)(6) |
| :--- |

[redacted]

USCENTCOM MDR 26-0038 to MDR 26-0046 Approved for Release to AARO 03/27/26 000001
cover_up_reasoning: The cover-up score is 30 because the document contains routine redactions of personal identifying information (b)(6) and operational details (unit, specific coordinates, internal contact info). While the document was previously classified, its release indicates a degree of transparency. There is no evidence of substantial concealment such as classified annexes, internal contradictions with public statements, or language suggesting witness intimidation. The redactions are standard for FOIA releases of military operational documents.
title_zh: DOW-UAP-D44，靶场干扰物报告表，亚丁湾，2020年10月
summary_zh: 一名美国军事操作员报告称，于2020年10月15日14:18:39Z至14:19:52Z期间，在亚丁湾上空通过红外传感器观察到一个“圆形、低温物体”。该物体被追踪到以约20英里/小时的速度向319度（西北方向）移动，并在约1分钟的接触期间进行了几次突然的方向改变。红外传感器设置为“黑热”模式，该物体呈现亮白色。传感器瞄准低于飞机高度-50度，斜距为4.06海里，地距为4.78公里。
reported_object_description_zh: 一个圆形、低温物体，在红外（黑热设置）下呈现亮白色。
reported_behavior_zh: 以20英里/小时的速度向319度（西北方向）移动，并进行突然的方向改变。
bust_reasoning_zh: 驳斥评分为45分，因为该文件描述了一个具有异常特征的物体（“圆形、低温物体”以20英里/小时的速度进行“突然的方向改变”），这些特征难以用简单的气象气球等常见平实解释来轻易说明，且缺乏进一步细节。红外图像中的“低温”特征是独特的。然而，这是一次单传感器（红外）观测，且只有一名目击者，且速度（20英里/小时）相对较低，使得更高级的运动学异常不太可能发生。小型无人机甚至大型鸟类等普通解释仍属合理，但文件并未明确识别出其中任何一种，因此将其归类为“真正不确定”范围。
cover_up_reasoning_zh: 掩盖评分为30分，因为该文件包含对个人身份信息（b)(6)）的常规编辑以及作战细节（单位、具体坐标、内部联系信息）的编辑。尽管该文件此前曾被列为机密，但其发布表明了一定程度的透明度。没有证据表明存在实质性隐瞒，例如机密附件、与公开声明的内部矛盾，或暗示恐吓证人的措辞。这些编辑是军事行动文件根据《信息自由法》发布时的标准做法。
claims: A round, cold object was observed via infrared sensor.
The object traveled at 319 degrees at 20 mph.
The object made abrupt directional changes.
claims_zh: 通过红外传感器观察到一个圆形、低温物体。
该物体以20英里/小时的速度向319度方向移动。
该物体进行了突然的方向改变。
bust_explanations: Small drone/UAS: A small drone could be round, travel at 20 mph, and make abrupt directional changes. Its thermal signature could appear 'cold' (bright white in black hot IR) depending on its construction, power state, and the ambient conditions, especially if it's small and not generating significant heat.

Balloon: A weather or surveillance balloon could be round and appear 'cold' in IR. While 'abrupt directional changes' are less typical for a passive balloon, they could occur due to wind shear or if it's a controlled, steerable balloon. The 20 mph speed is plausible for a balloon in wind.

Large bird: A large bird could appear somewhat 'round' in IR, fly at 20 mph, and make abrupt directional changes. Birds often appear 'cold' in IR as their surface temperature can be close to ambient or cooler than the background in certain conditions.
bust_explanations_zh: 小型无人机/无人航空系统：小型无人机可以是圆形的，以20英里/小时的速度飞行，并进行突然的方向改变。其热特征可能显示为“低温”（在黑热红外模式下呈亮白色），具体取决于其结构、电源状态和环境条件，特别是如果它很小且不产生大量热量。
气球：气象气球或侦察气球可以是圆形的，并在红外图像中显示为“低温”。虽然“突然的方向改变”对于被动气球来说不太典型，但它们可能因风切变或如果它是受控的可操纵气球而发生。20英里/小时的速度对于在风中的气球来说是合理的。
大型鸟类：大型鸟类在红外图像中可能显得有些“圆形”，以20英里/小时的速度飞行，并进行突然的方向改变。鸟类在红外图像中通常显示为“低温”，因为在某些条件下，它们的体表温度可能接近环境温度或低于背景温度。
cover_up_indicators: Routine redaction of personal identifying information (b)(6)
Redaction of operational details (unit, specific location coordinates)
cover_up_indicators_zh: 对个人身份信息（b)(6)）的常规编辑
对作战细节（单位、具体位置坐标）的编辑
文件此前曾被列为机密
